title: boerhavia diffusa var. minor
intro: Boerhavia diffusa var. minor, also known as dwarf spiderwort or lesser snakeroot, is a perennial plant species within the Nyctaginaceae family, commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant properties. It is believed to provide various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating pain, and treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Boerhavia diffusa var. minor can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine to treat various health conditions.
